name: Federal Reserve
summary: Day 78 of Hormuz blockade. **KEVIN WARSH IS NOW FED CHAIR — EFFECTIVE MAY 16 (TODAY)** replacing Powell, confirmed by Senate 54-45 on May 13 (CONFIRMED multi-source primary — CNN, NPR, ABA, C-SPAN, WaPo, CNBC). Warsh wants to shrink the balance sheet and rely on traditional rate tool; his preference for trimmed-mean inflation measures means he sees underlying inflation cooler than headline, but his balance-sheet-shrinking inclination makes him potentially more hawkish on the QE channel. **'Investors now expecting Fed to keep rate unchanged for the rest of the year — or even raise rates if inflation worsens'** (Yahoo Finance May 13). Trump allies warning Warsh on rate cuts (WaPo May 14). **APRIL PPI +1.4% m/m / +6% YoY (BLS May 13)** = biggest single-month jump since December 2022; Core PPI +1% (vs 0.4% est); ~60% of rise was in services; energy at the root. Combined with April CPI 3.8% YoY (May 12) the Fed easing path is **closed indefinitely**. Morgan Stanley moved rate-cut call to 2027 earlier in the week; CNBC/Yahoo reporting markets now consider rate hike possible. **STAGFLATION-REGIME BOND ROUT ON MAY 15**: 10Y +14bp to 4.595% (highest since Feb 2025), 30Y +11bp to 5.121% (highest since May 2025) — Bloomberg labelled it 'the worst weekly Treasury rout in a year.' Markets uncertain about Warsh's reaction function for first 90 days = term premium expanding. **Cross-asset confirmation**: silver -9.1% (Goldman: dual identity hit hardest), gold -2.6% (real-yield headwind), DXY +0.4% to 99.27, S&P -1.24%, VIX 17.26→18.43. The pre-war Rieder-dovish-regime hopium (gold +2.7% May 6 partly on Rieder speculation) is fully priced out. PRIOR (May 13): **A2 APRIL CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS RELEASE MAY 12) — THREE-YEAR HIGH, HIGHEST SINCE MAY 2023, +0.6% MoM matching expectations; CORE +2.8% YoY / +0.4% MoM (highest monthly print since Jan 2025); ENERGY contributed >40% of headline gain (+3.8% MoM, +17.9% YoY); GASOLINE +28.4% YoY; SHELTER +0.6%. BLS itself flagged that inflation is 'a problem beyond the Iran war impacts.'** **FIRST CYCLE INFLATION PRINT TO FULLY REFLECT OIL PASS-THROUGH — directly closes the door on near-term Fed easing.** US 10Y bid at 4.46% (+5bps, +1.2% 1D); real-yield channel tightens; gold +0.8% only despite 3-year-high inflation (real-yield channel competing with safe-haven bid); URA -5.0% (positioning unwind). S&P 500 closed -0.29% May 12 at 7,392 (off May 11's 7,412.84 record); NASDAQ followed. Fed cannot cut while Brent stays above $100 — the 90%-confidence trigger ('Brent sustained >$100 for 4+ weeks') activated April 22 is fully visible in macro data today. Fed-rate-cut-driven US equity rally thesis is OUT for 2026 unless either Brent breaks $95 sustained OR core inflation reverses below 2.5% — neither imminent. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: energy contribution (~40% of CPI gain) reverses on oil retrace; core sticky. The Fed's pre-war assumption that energy would be 'transitory' is now hostage to whether Brent sustains above $100 through Q2 reporting. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 10): **DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M); RIEDER FED-CHAIR SPECULATION + TRUMP COMFORTABLE-WITH-WEAK-DOLLAR COMMENTS = DOLLAR-WEAKNESS REGIME INTACT.** US 10Y 4.36% (-1.4% 1D, +0.3% 1M). VIX 17.39 cycle-low. Today's metals rally (gold +1.1%, silver +2.9%, copper +1.0%) is dollar-driven not war-risk — cross-asset confirmation: VIX low, S&P record, defense weak, yields lower. **BlackRock's Rick Rieder (vocal advocate of aggressive rate cuts) emerging as leading candidate for next Fed chair → dovish-Fed expectation builds.** If Rieder confirmed/announced as next Fed chair, dollar-weakness regime confirmed → multi-quarter EM-bullish setup including Indian equities. Brent collapse to ~$103 May 6 + World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman Q2 $90 reduces inflation pass-through pressure on Fed; Powell's April 'it hasn't even peaked yet' framing now structurally contradicted by post-MOU energy-price-decline trajectory. CME June cut probability ~28%; Polymarket ~56% zero 2026 cuts — but the rate path could now reprice dovish if MOU signs and Brent settles at $86-90. **The Fed's biggest single dovish catalyst is now MOU signature** — institutional energy-inflation acceptance becomes inverse of the kinetic-restart pricing.

recent_signals.headline: A2 APRIL CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS MAY 12) = THREE-YEAR HIGH (highest since May 2023); +0.6% MoM matching expectations; CORE +2.8% YoY / +0.4% MoM (highest monthly print since Jan 2025); ENERGY contributed >40% of headline gain (+3.8% MoM, +17.9% YoY); GASOLINE +28.4% YoY; SHELTER +0.6%. BLS itself flagged inflation 'a problem beyond Iran war impacts.' First cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through. US 10Y bid at 4.46% (+5bps); real-yield channel tightens; gold +0.8% only on 3-year-high CPI (real-yield channel competing with safe-haven bid); URA -5.0% (positioning unwind). S&P 500 -0.29% May 12 to 7,392 (off May 11 record). Fed cannot cut while Brent >$100 — 90%-confidence trigger ('Brent sustained >$100 for 4+ weeks') activated April 22 now fully visible in macro data. Fed-rate-cut-driven US equity rally thesis OUT for 2026 unless either Brent breaks $95 sustained OR core inflation reverses <2.5% — neither imminent. Fed-speak embargo through May 15.
sources: CNN Business 'US inflation rose to 3.8% in April'
recent_signals.verification: CPI 3.8% YoY = CONFIRMED (BLS primary statistic, multi-source); core +2.8% / +0.4% MoM = CONFIRMED (BLS primary); energy contribution >40% of headline gain = CONFIRMED (BLS data); US 10Y +5bps = CONFIRMED tape (yfinance).

historical_summaries.summary: 4 signals. FOMC voted 11-1 to hold rates at 3 | Market removed basically every rate cut for 2026, now pricing odds of a HIKE | Fed remains trapped -- 10Y at 4
19 signals. Eurozone inflation surged to 2 | Fed trapped in stagflation -- ISM Prices Index at 78 | DXY fell to 99
